questions.text: What does TOR stand for?
When was the TOR code made freely available?
What type of network does TOR build?
options: An overlay network on top of the Internet

questions.text: Which part of the Internet is NOT publicly accessible and requires special software like TOR?
In a typical TOR circuit, how many nodes does traffic pass through?
What does the Entry Node (Guard Node) know about a connection?
options: The IP of the client and the destination
Only the IP of the destination
Only the IP of the client
Neither the client nor the destination IP

questions.text: What does the Exit Node know about a connection?
options: The IP of the client and the destination
Only the IP of the client
Only the IP of the destination
Neither the client nor the destination IP

questions.text: What is the purpose of Bridge Nodes in the TOR network?
options: To provide entry points in countries where TOR is blocked

questions.text: What is the fixed size of TOR cells (data packets exchanged between nodes)?
Which of the following is NOT covered by TOR's threat model?
options: An attacker observing part of the communication
An attacker controlling some TOR nodes
An attacker controlling both the Entry Guard and Exit Node
An attacker running a single compromised relay

questions.text: What is the pseudo top-level domain used for Onion Services?
For Onion Services, is HTTPS necessary for secure communication?
options: Yes, HTTPS is always required for security
No, HTTP is sufficient because TOR encrypts the traffic
Only for Onion Services handling payments
Yes, because Exit Nodes can read HTTP traffic

questions.text: Who performs DNS lookups in the TOR network?
How many Directory Authorities existed as of 2023?
What is a key difference between TOR and a VPN?
options: TOR is centralized, VPN is decentralized
VPN providers know the client's IP, TOR Exit Nodes do not
TOR requires subscription fees, VPNs are free

questions.text: What is an Exit Policy in TOR?
options: A rule that determines which nodes can be entry guards
A configuration that defines which traffic a node will forward to the non-TOR internet
A security policy for TOR developers
A law enforcement requirement for exit node operators

questions.text: Which of the following is a correct description of the TOR Browser's security levels?
What is the main principle behind Onion Routing?
options: Data is encrypted only at the transport layer
Data packets are encrypted multiple times, each node removes one layer
Data is split into small onion-shaped fragments
Each node re-encrypts the data before forwarding

questions.text: What is the role of Introduction Points in Onion Services?
options: They encrypt the data between client and server
They act as intermediaries for connection requests to the Onion Service
They block malicious connections to Onion Services

questions.text: Which of the following are types of TOR nodes? (Select all that apply)
Estimate the minimum bandwidth (in KB/s) required for a TOR node to be classified as 'fast'.
